samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, the motor vehicle lane light should be green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. The lane light and the sidewalk light should be paired: when the lane light is green or yellow, the sidewalk light is red; when the lane light is red, the sidewalk light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1_red">State 1, red signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2_yellow">State 2, yellow signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3_green">State 3, green signal.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>
                State transition logic:
                - In the idle state, transition to the s1_red state.
                - In the s1_red state, transition to the s3_green state after 3 clock cycles.
                - In the s2_yellow state, transition to the s1_red state after 3 clock cycles.
                - In the s3_green state, transition to the s2_yellow state after 3 clock cycles.
            </description>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <description>
                Counter logic:
                - On a negative edge of the reset signal, the counter is set to 10.
                - If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10.
                - If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60.
                - If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5.
                - If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10.
                - Otherwise, the counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock.
            </description>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. Here's a summary of each state:
            - idle: Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to the s1_red state immediately.
            - s1_red: Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to the s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in the s1_red state.
            - s2_yellow: Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to the s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in the s2_yellow state.
            - s3_green: Sets the green signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to the s2_yellow state. Otherwise, it remains in the s3_green state.
        </description>
        <implementation id="state">
            always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
                if (!rst_n) begin
                    state <= idle;
                end
                else begin
                    case (state)
                        idle: begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                        s1_red: begin
                            if (cnt == 3) begin
                                state <= s3_green;
                            end
                            else begin
                                state <= s1_red;
                            end
                        end
                        s2_yellow: begin
                            if (cnt == 3) begin
                                state <= s1_red;
                            end
                            else begin
                                state <= s2_yellow;
                            end
                        end
                        s3_green: begin
                            if (cnt == 3) begin
                                state <= s2_yellow;
                            end
                            else begin
                                state <= s3_green;
                            end
                        end
                    endcase
                end
            end
        </implementation>
        <description>
            The second always block handles the counting logic of the internal counter (cnt). The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on various conditions:
            - If (!rst_n), the counter is set to 10.
            - If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10.
            - If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60.
            - If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5.
            - If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10.
            - Otherwise, the counter is decremented normally.
        </description>
        <implementation id="cnt">
            always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
                if (!rst_n) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end
                else begin
                    if (pass_request && green) begin
                        cnt <= 10;
                    end
                    else if (!green && p_green) begin
                        cnt <= 60;
                    end
                    else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                        cnt <= 5;
                    end
                    else if (!red && p_red) begin
                        cnt <= 10;
                    end
                    else begin
                        cnt <= cnt - 1;
                    end
                end
            end
        </implementation>
        <description>
            The assign statement assigns the value of the internal counter (cnt) to the output clock.
        </description>
        <implementation id="clock">
            assign clock = cnt;
        </implementation>
        <description>
            The final always block handles the output signals. It assigns the previous values (p_red, p_yellow, p_green) to the output signals (red, yellow, green) on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
        </description>
        <implementation id="output">
            always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
                if (!rst_n) begin
                    red <= 0;
                    yellow <= 0;
                    green <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    red <= p_red;
                    yellow <= p_yellow;
                    green <= p_green;
                end
            end
        </implementation>
    </implementation>
</module>
